ogTitle: Old Tallangatta vs Ombersley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Old Tallangatta and Ombersley.
heading: Old Tallangatta vs Ombersley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Old Tallangatta, Victoria and Ombersley, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Ombersley has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Old Tallangatta (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Old Tallangatta or Ombersley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Old Tallangatta has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Ombersley scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Old Tallangatta scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Ombersley scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Old Tallangatta or Ombersley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Ombersley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
